MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-14-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. EDWARD A. JUDGE, Esq. of Law Office of Edward A. Judge (Tel: 415-391-2447) for plaintiff;
Atty. HENRY A. WIRTA, JR., Esq. of Harrington, Foxx, Dubrow & Canter, LLP (Tel: 415-565-9600) for defendants Kaura Agnew, D.C. and Agnew Family Chiropractic.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF BREANNA GUNN's Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's "motion to file first amended complaint for negligence and lack of informed consent" is granted. That trial courts are to liberally permit amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP sec. 428.50(c).) Generally, a proposed amended complaint's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
